title: i want to build a trading ai expert with openclaw
author: Itchy_Permission_456
selftext:
iv been messing with openclaw for about 2-3 weeks now and i was really impressed. i wanted to make an ai trading expert that doesn't trade for me but tells me what i could trade based off 5 scout bots i made that each had their own subject to constantly monitor on. so the original idea was supposed to be having collecting recent raw data such as Tweets/News/Company's/government/ect. all this info would go to the Analyzer that would filter out and score with a Algorythim i set runs through six weighted factors: who said it, where it came from, how actionable it is, how old it is, whether we found it first, and how clear the direction is. Late news gets penalized hard and ect. after being added to the data base it then goes to the detective where its goal is to repeatedly ask questions trying to link factors together and the strategist would learn and adapt. after it goes to a signal room with a confidence % on which stock to by and when to sell. i guess what i meant to say is this is impossible for my skill that is. but every time i go more into detail the bots seem to get to overcomplicated or just doesint work how i intended it to. just wanna seek some advice on what i could do and what layout can actually work. also i need friends 

LAYER 1 — COLLECTION    Scouts (×5)

>Celebrity — famous people move markets

>Congress — politicians trade before votes

>Macro — fed and economic data

>Earnings — companies beat or miss

>Insider — executives buying their own stock

LAYER 2 — PROCESSING    Analyzer

LAYER 3 — INTELLIGENCE  Database

LAYER 4 — REASONING     Detective ↔ Strategist

LAYER 5 — OUTPUT        Signal Room

LAYER 6 — DECISION      Me

comments.author: ChampionshipBorn496
comments.body: That can be a good idea if executed well.

comments.author: StevenVinyl
comments.body: there's already an app doing this, take inspiration, called Cod3x

replies.author: Itchy_Permission_456
replies.body: Just took a look at it and it’s really nice and greatly built. From what I’m seeing it’s mostly chart strategy based Tho

replies.author: StevenVinyl
replies.body: Social UI (X, Youtube, etc...) coming soon. But you can plug in your own API, MCP, GraphQL, etc.. sources as well.

comments.author: Tomatillo_Known
comments.body:
[https://clawhub.ai/phutt-bwai/zonein](https://clawhub.ai/phutt-bwai/zonein)

  
My friend built a skill to track and analyze whales/top performers on Hyperliquid, its free and will save you time for your intelligence database

comments.author: Murrrrcy
comments.body: What model are you using for your agent? I was considering OpenAI's Codex 5.2 but wanted to use Llama 3B

comments.author: ok-hacker
comments.body: That multi-layer architecture is smart. We took a similar approach with andmilo—building strategy and risk management as explicit layers rather than hoping the LLM figures it out. Pattern recognition and regime detection work way better with deterministic rules on top.
